task_completion.description: Navigate to the email or receipt settings in Copper Point of Sale
task_completion.visual_evidence: A settings or options window in Copper POS is open, with the 'Email', 'Receipts', or 'SMTP' configuration section active.

task_completion.description: Enter the correct SMTP Host and Port
task_completion.visual_evidence: The SMTP Host or Server field contains 'smtp.sendgrid.net' and the Port field contains '587'.
Checkboxes or dropdowns for 'Secure Connection' (SSL/TLS/STARTTLS) and 'Authentication' (or 'Server requires authentication') are checked or enabled.

task_completion.description: Enter the SMTP Username and Password
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Username field contains the exact string 'apikey' and the Password field contains text or masked characters (dots/asterisks).

task_completion.description: Enter the Sender and Reply-To email addresses
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Sender/From Email field contains 'receipts@citylightsbooks.com' and the Reply-To field contains 'manager@citylightsbooks.com'.
The Email Subject or Subject Template field contains exactly 'Your City Lights Bookstore Receipt'.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the Copper POS graphical user interface
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the actual Copper Point of Sale application windows and dialogs, not a terminal window, text editor, or script modifying configuration files directly.

integrity.description: Verify the inputs are genuinely entered into the application
integrity.visual_evidence: The text should be visible inside the standard input fields of the Copper POS settings window, without signs of image manipulation or pasting into a mock interface.

integrity.description: Verify the agent attempts to save or apply the configuration
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent should click 'OK', 'Save', or 'Apply' after entering the settings, rather than just typing them and closing the window without saving.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The correct SendGrid SMTP host is 'smtp.sendgrid.net', the recommended TLS port is '587', and the required SendGrid authentication username is the literal string 'apikey'.
